patterns_recognized: HARD CAP 7 sub-0.02 false-negative band: hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap in [0.00, 0.02] produced three consecutive incorrect suppressions (KC@BAL 7/10 +0.018, 7/12 +0.0189; MIA@MIL 7/18 +0.0185); pipeline trigger threshold should be raised from >=0 to >=0.02 before next calibration cycle.

F5-only signal without F1/F3 corroboration is an unreliable GOLD anchor: LAD@NYY three-day run (7/17 NYY lost 2-1, 7/18 postponed, 7/19 same config) where F5_bullpen_gap alone without F3 >= 1000 was correctly DOWNGRADEd each time.

BAL-series caution at HOU requires the same opposing-signal shape (SP-xERA against + PQI against); today all signals favor HOU — the caution condition from the 7/18 postgame is explicitly NOT met and does not extend to games where the signal shape is inverted.

D-grade dual-opposing picks (SP-xERA against + PQI against) are stake-suppression signals only, not directional fades — SKIP is always correct; OVERRIDE toward the opposing team is never warranted per repeated 7/9-7/18 archive lessons.

Stage 2 full-game direction outperforms Stage 1 F5 direction when |f5_full_delta| >= 0.12, UNLESS |pqi_diff| > 20 opposing, in which case PQI becomes the dominant directional signal (7/17 STL@ARI lesson validated).

CHW @ TOR.reasoning: Edge +1.21pp below the 4pp floor; HARD CAP 10 fired (pick_prob=0.5001, non-CONFIRM bottom bucket). Pre-cap score only 2 — PQI +6.2 and Stage agree, no F-signal or SP-xERA in grade stack. TOR beat CHW 1-0 on 7/18 confirming directional validity, but the signal stack lacks the four-dimension corroboration required for elevation. No live context changes the call.

LAD @ NYY (G1).reasoning: F5_bullpen_gap=+0.98 is the sole firing signal — no F1 or F3 corroboration. This is identical to the F5-only-without-F3 pattern correctly DOWNGRADEd on 7/17 (NYY lost 2-1) and carried unresolved from 7/18 postponement. No independent lineup conviction signal. HARD CAP 10 already zeroed the score; confidence_downgrade=True; hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0176 in the sub-0.02 false-negative band. DOWNGRADE to SKIP.

TB @ BOS.reasoning: HARD CAP 7 fired on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0117, squarely in the documented sub-0.02 false-negative band (three consecutive false negatives 7/10-7/18: +0.018, +0.0189, +0.0185). Pre-cap A- (SP 0.38 xERA, PQI +8.3, Stage agree, score=4) is a solid directional stack with Sonny Gray. Not overriding the cap absent live qualitative news — this is a pipeline calibration issue the 7/18 postgame flagged for future threshold revision, not a per-game override mandate.

NYM @ PHI.reasoning: Score 2 (B) — below GOLD threshold (score >= 3 required). PQI delta=-5.5 and Stage agree are the only affirming signals; no F-signal or SP-xERA agreement. Alan Rangel (PHI) vs Nolan McLean (NYM): no documented xERA edge in grade_reasons. PHI crushed NYM 6-1 on 7/18 but that game had Luzardo's real SP edge; today's matchup lacks independent corroboration for elevation.

TEX @ ATL.reasoning: D-grade dual-opposing: SP 0.62 xERA AGAINST ATL (Eovaldi/TEX has the edge over Holmes/ATL) and PQI delta=-5.7 (TEX has better team quality). TEX beat ATL 7-6 on 7/18 in this same series. Archive rule: D-grade dual-opposing is strict SKIP — no directional OVERRIDE toward TEX warranted per 7/11 pattern guidance. D-grade signals bet-suppression, not a directional fade.

PIT @ CLE.reasoning: D-grade dual-opposing: SP 1.17 xERA AGAINST CLE (Paul Skenes/PIT has a decisive edge over Joey Cantillo/CLE) and PQI delta=-12.7 (PIT has better team quality). Skenes is an elite ace; the gap is real. PIT won 7-1 in G1 on 7/18. Clean dual-opposing SKIP per 7/11 archive; no OVERRIDE toward PIT warranted.

BAL @ HOU.reasoning: Pre-cap score 5 (A): F5_bullpen_gap=+0.68 fires, SP 0.47 xERA agrees with HOU, PQI delta=+6.0 confirms, Stage agree (delta=0.00). Edge 12.66pp in valid [4,15]pp range. The 7/17-7/18 BAL-series caution does NOT apply today: both prior losses had SP-xERA and PQI opposing HOU; today both favor HOU — signal shape is inverted. bullpen_fatigue stress warning noted; confidence_downgrade=True but Stage delta=0.0174 well below CAP 4. F-signal + SP + PQI meets the GOLD-CONFIRM bar at the 65% boundary per 7/17 lesson.

SD @ KC.reasoning: D-grade: SP 0.73 xERA AGAINST SD (Cameron/KC better than Marquez/SD). f5_full_delta=0.1246 with Stage 1 giving KC 59.33% F5 while Stage 2 flips to SD full-game — classic Stage 1/2 disagreement zone. PQI delta=-1.85 is neutral. hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0389 favors SD bullpen but the SP disadvantage and Stage 1/2 disagree override. KC blew out SD 6-1 on 7/18.

MIA @ MIL.reasoning: Three independent zero-stake triggers: (1) HARD CAP 7 fires on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0185 (same sub-0.02 false-negative band as 7/10, 7/12, 7/18); (2) HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket (pick_prob=0.550); (3) edge 3.8pp below 4pp floor. Pre-cap A- (F3=3225.7, F5=+0.86, SP 0.34, Stage agree) is directionally solid, but triple suppression plus confidence_downgrade=True and acute_roster warning prevent any stake. Not overriding without live news.

MIN @ CHC.reasoning: D-grade: SP 0.84 xERA AGAINST MIN (Shota Imanaga/CHC has the edge over Matthews/MIN) and PQI delta=+13.8 (CHC has better team quality). f5_full_delta=0.1225 with Stage 1 giving CHC 60.47% F5. Imanaga is an elite starter with a real advantage. 7/17 MIN won 5-2 against CHC pick; 7/18 CHC won 6-2 — series 1-1. Today MIN is picked against Imanaga with dual opposing signals: SKIP.

CIN @ COL.reasoning: B+ (score 3): F5_bullpen_gap=+1.77 fires, PQI delta=-11.7 confirms CIN, Stage agree. hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.0208 (CIN has bullpen quality edge). Hunter Greene (CIN away) vs Ryan Feltner (COL home) at Coors. Edge 3.4pp below 4pp floor so stake_mult=0. 7/17 postgame flagged this exact edge-floor DOWNGRADE as the archive's strongest false-negative candidate (CIN won 7-2); 7/18 Coors volatility noted. Directional case for CIN is sound; stake already correctly zero via edge floor.

WSH @ OAK.reasoning: Negative edge -1.23pp (HARD CAP 1 adjacent — market says WSH is overvalued). PQI delta=+5.5 (OAK has modest team quality edge opposing WSH). HARD CAP 10 fired. WSH@OAK is extreme-variance: 7/17 WSH won 23-4; 7/18 OAK won 15-1 in consecutive days of the same series. confidence_downgrade=True. Do not use either prior result as a directional prior per 7/18 postgame warning.

DET @ LAA.reasoning: D-grade: SP 1.89 xERA AGAINST LAA (Casey Mize/DET decisively better than Ryan Johnson/LAA) and PQI delta=-22.6 (large DET quality advantage). fair_prob=0.3917 below 0.42 viability floor; edge +17.53pp above 15pp ceiling. 7/18: DET shut out LAA 7-0 with this same extreme multi-signal opposition shape. Directional lean is sharply toward DET.

SF @ SEA.reasoning: D-grade: SP 0.82 xERA AGAINST SF (Logan Gilbert/SEA edge over Robbie Ray/SF) and PQI delta=+9.6 (SEA home better team quality). fair_prob=0.3579 well below 0.42 viability floor; edge +19.26pp above 15pp ceiling with calibration_caution_18_25pp flag (Soft CAP 6.5). 7/17 SEA beat SF 4-3; 7/18 SEA beat SF 4-3 again — two consecutive SF losses with the same signal shape.

STL @ ARI.reasoning: D-grade: SP 1.32 xERA AGAINST ARI (Pallante/STL edge over E.Rodriguez/ARI) and PQI delta=-18.8 (STL has better team quality). bullpen_fatigue + confidence_downgrade=True. 7/18 ARI won 5-3 despite weaker opposing signals (0.68 xERA + PQI -6.5); today opposition is materially stronger. PQI approaching but below the >20 threshold where PQI dominates Stage 2 per 7/17 lesson; Stage 1/2 agree (delta=0.04) here anyway. SKIP confirmed.

LAD @ NYY (G2).reasoning: PENDING_SP_DATA — neither LAD nor NYY probable SP announced for game 2. Pipeline correctly withholds a directional call pending 100+ Statcast pitches from both starters. No scoring possible. Confirm the hold; fill in at next workflow run when SPs are declared.
